source: Curated, literature-cited reference (cad/resistance.py) — textbook on-target clinical resistance.

mutations.covered_by: osimertinib (3rd-gen) — this is why osimertinib mattered

mutations.confers: resistance to osimertinib (3rd-gen), especially in cis with T790M
mutations.covered_by: no approved TKI fully covers it — 4th-gen inhibitors are still investigational
mutations.ref: Wang et al., J Hematol Oncol 2016

targets.unmet: a molecule covering C797S (the post-osimertinib gap) is a live, named unmet need.

targets.context: CLL / lymphoma, on covalent BTK inhibitors

mutations.confers: resistance to covalent BTKis (ibrutinib, acalabrutinib, zanubrutinib) by removing the covalent-binding cysteine
mutations.covered_by: pirtobrutinib (non-covalent) — active regardless of C481S status
no approved agent — an open frontier

targets.unmet: agents covering post-pirtobrutinib (T474 / kinase-dead) BTK resistance are an open frontier.

mutations.confers: the 'gatekeeper' mutation — resistance to imatinib and all 2nd-gen TKIs (dasatinib, nilotinib, bosutinib)

targets.unmet: ponatinib+asciminib-resistant compound mutants (e.g. T315M, E255V/T315I) remain hard.

mutations.confers: solvent-front mutation — resistance to 1st/2nd-gen ALK TKIs (crizotinib, alectinib, ceritinib, brigatinib)
mutations.covered_by: lorlatinib (3rd-gen), designed to retain activity against G1202R
mutations.ref: Gainor et al., Cancer Discov 2016; Shaw, NEJM 2020 (lorlatinib)

targets.unmet: lorlatinib-resistant compound mutations (e.g. G1202R + L1196M) are the next gap.

targets.context: KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC / colorectal, on covalent G12C inhibitors

mutations.confers: resistance to covalent G12C inhibitors (sotorasib, adagrasib) — a switch-II-pocket mutation that blocks inhibitor binding
mutations.covered_by: no approved agent — next-gen tri-complex G12C inhibitors (e.g. RMC-6291) and RAS(ON) inhibitors are still investigational
mutations.ref: Tanaka et al., Cancer Discov 2021 (adagrasib Y96D); Awad et al., NEJM 2021

targets.unmet: on-target G12C resistance (Y96 switch-II pocket, secondary RAS mutations) and convergent RAS-MAPK reactivation are largely uncovered by approved agents — a live frontier for next-gen and RAS(ON) inhibitors.

disclaimer: Curated, literature-cited; scoped to textbook on-target clinical resistance — not exhaustive, not a fetched dataset. Resistance is often polyclonal and pathway-level too, and the absence of a target here is not a claim it has none. Research only; not medical advice.
